4615		 * caller must have AccessExclusiveLock on the relation, or some other
4616		 * reason to be certain that no one is loading new pages of the rel
4617		 * into the buffer pool.  (Otherwise we might well miss such pages
4618		 * entirely.)  Therefore, while the tag might be changing while we
4619		 * look at it, it can't be changing *to* a value we care about, only
4620		 * *away* from such a value.  So false negatives are impossible, and
4621		 * false positives are safe because we'll recheck after getting the
4622		 * buffer lock.
4623		 *
4624		 * We could check forkNum and blockNum as well as the rlocator, but
4625		 * the incremental win from doing so seems small.
4626		 */
4627		if (!BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rlocator.locator))
4628			continue;
4629
4630		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
4631
4632		for (j = 0; j < nforks; j++)
4633		{
4634			if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rlocator.locator) &&
4635				BufTagGetForkNum(&bufHdr->tag) == forkNum[j] &&
4636				bufHdr->tag.blockNum >= firstDelBlock[j])
4637			{
4638				InvalidateBuffer(bufHdr);	/* releases spinlock */
4639				break;
4640			}
4641		}
4642		if (j >= nforks)
4643			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
4644	}
4645}
4646
4647/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
4648 *		DropRelationsAllBuffers
4649 *
4650 *		This function removes from the buffer pool all the pages of all
4651 *		forks of the specified relations.  It's equivalent to calling
4652 *		DropRelationBuffers once per fork per relation with firstDelBlock = 0.
4653 *		--------------------------------------------------------------------
4654 */
4655void
4656DropRelationsAllBuffers(SMgrRelation *smgr_reln, int nlocators)
4657{
4658	int			i;
4659	int			n = 0;
4660	SMgrRelation *rels;
4661	BlockNumber (*block)[MAX_FORKNUM + 1];
4662	uint64		nBlocksToInvalidate = 0;
4663	RelFileLocator *locators;
4664	bool		cached = true;
4665	bool		use_bsearch;
4666
4667	if (nlocators == 0)
4668		return;
4669
4670	rels = palloc(sizeof(SMgrRelation) * nlocators);	/* non-local relations */
4671
4672	/* If it's a local relation, it's localbuf.c's problem. */
4673	for (i = 0; i < nlocators; i++)
4674	{
4675		if (RelFileLocatorBackendIsTemp(smgr_reln[i]->smgr_rlocator))
4676		{
4677			if (smgr_reln[i]->smgr_rlocator.backend == MyProcNumber)
4678				DropRelationAllLocalBuffers(smgr_reln[i]->smgr_rlocator.locator);
4679		}
4680		else
4681			rels[n++] = smgr_reln[i];
4682	}
4683
4684	/*
4685	 * If there are no non-local relations, then we're done. Release the
4686	 * memory and return.
4687	 */
4688	if (n == 0)
4689	{
4690		pfree(rels);
4691		return;
4692	}
4693
4694	/*
4695	 * This is used to remember the number of blocks for all the relations
4696	 * forks.
4697	 */
4698	block = (BlockNumber (*)[MAX_FORKNUM + 1])
4699		palloc(sizeof(BlockNumber) * n * (MAX_FORKNUM + 1));
4700
4701	/*
4702	 * We can avoid scanning the entire buffer pool if we know the exact size
4703	 * of each of the given relation forks. See DropRelationBuffers.
4704	 */
4705	for (i = 0; i < n && cached; i++)
4706	{
4707		for (int j = 0; j <= MAX_FORKNUM; j++)
4708		{
4709			/* Get the number of blocks for a relation's fork. */
4710			block[i][j] = smgrnblocks_cached(rels[i], j);
4711
4712			/* We need to only consider the relation forks that exists. */
4713			if (block[i][j] == InvalidBlockNumber)
4714			{
4715				if (!smgrexists(rels[i], j))
4716					continue;
4717				cached = false;
4718				break;
4719			}
4720
4721			/* calculate the total number of blocks to be invalidated */
4722			nBlocksToInvalidate += block[i][j];
4723		}
4724	}
4725
4726	/*
4727	 * We apply the optimization iff the total number of blocks to invalidate
4728	 * is below the BUF_DROP_FULL_SCAN_THRESHOLD.
4729	 */
4730	if (cached && nBlocksToInvalidate < BUF_DROP_FULL_SCAN_THRESHOLD)
4731	{
4732		for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
4733		{
4734			for (int j = 0; j <= MAX_FORKNUM; j++)
4735			{
4736				/* ignore relation forks that doesn't exist */
4737				if (!BlockNumberIsValid(block[i][j]))
4738					continue;
4739
4740				/* drop all the buffers for a particular relation fork */
4741				FindAndDropRelationBuffers(rels[i]->smgr_rlocator.locator,
4742										   j, block[i][j], 0);
4743			}
4744		}
4745
4746		pfree(block);
4747		pfree(rels);
4748		return;
4749	}
4750
4751	pfree(block);
4752	locators = palloc(sizeof(RelFileLocator) * n);	/* non-local relations */
4753	for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
4754		locators[i] = rels[i]->smgr_rlocator.locator;
4755
4756	/*
4757	 * For low number of relations to drop just use a simple walk through, to
4758	 * save the bsearch overhead. The threshold to use is rather a guess than
4759	 * an exactly determined value, as it depends on many factors (CPU and RAM
4760	 * speeds, amount of shared buffers etc.).
4761	 */
4762	use_bsearch = n > RELS_BSEARCH_THRESHOLD;
4763
4764	/* sort the list of rlocators if necessary */
4765	if (use_bsearch)
4766		qsort(locators, n, sizeof(RelFileLocator), rlocator_comparator);
4767
4768	for (i = 0; i < NBuffers; i++)
4769	{
4770		RelFileLocator *rlocator = NULL;
4771		BufferDesc *bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(i);
4772		uint32		buf_state;
4773
4774		/*
4775		 * As in DropRelationBuffers, an unlocked precheck should be safe and
4776		 * saves some cycles.
4777		 */
4778
4779		if (!use_bsearch)
4780		{
4781			int			j;
4782
4783			for (j = 0; j < n; j++)
4784			{
4785				if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &locators[j]))
4786				{
4787					rlocator = &locators[j];
4788					break;
4789				}
4790			}
4791		}
4792		else
4793		{
4794			RelFileLocator locator;
4795
4796			locator = BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag);
4797			rlocator = bsearch(&locator,
4798							   locators, n, sizeof(RelFileLocator),
4799							   rlocator_comparator);
4800		}
4801
4802		/* buffer doesn't belong to any of the given relfilelocators; skip it */
4803		if (rlocator == NULL)
4804			continue;
4805
4806		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
4807		if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, rlocator))
4808			InvalidateBuffer(bufHdr);	/* releases spinlock */
4809		else
4810			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
4811	}
4812
4813	pfree(locators);
4814	pfree(rels);
4815}
4816
4817/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
4818 *		FindAndDropRelationBuffers
4819 *
4820 *		This function performs look up in BufMapping table and removes from the
4821 *		buffer pool all the pages of the specified relation fork that has block
4822 *		number >= firstDelBlock. (In particular, with firstDelBlock = 0, all
4823 *		pages are removed.)
4824 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
4825 */
4826static void
4827FindAndDropRelationBuffers(RelFileLocator rlocator, ForkNumber forkNum,
4828						   BlockNumber nForkBlock,
4829						   BlockNumber firstDelBlock)
4830{
4831	BlockNumber curBlock;
4832
4833	for (curBlock = firstDelBlock; curBlock < nForkBlock; curBlock++)
4834	{
4835		uint32		bufHash;	/* hash value for tag */
4836		BufferTag	bufTag;		/* identity of requested block */
4837		LWLock	   *bufPartitionLock;	/* buffer partition lock for it */
4838		int			buf_id;
4839		BufferDesc *bufHdr;
4840		uint32		buf_state;
4841
4842		/* create a tag so we can lookup the buffer */
4843		InitBufferTag(&bufTag, &rlocator, forkNum, curBlock);
4844
4845		/* determine its hash code and partition lock ID */
4846		bufHash = BufTableHashCode(&bufTag);
4847		bufPartitionLock = BufMappingPartitionLock(bufHash);
4848
4849		/* Check that it is in the buffer pool. If not, do nothing. */
4850		LWLockAcquire(bufPartitionLock, LW_SHARED);
4851		buf_id = BufTableLookup(&bufTag, bufHash);
4852		LWLockRelease(bufPartitionLock);
4853
4854		if (buf_id < 0)
4855			continue;
4856
4857		bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buf_id);
4858
4859		/*
4860		 * We need to lock the buffer header and recheck if the buffer is
4861		 * still associated with the same block because the buffer could be
4862		 * evicted by some other backend loading blocks for a different
4863		 * relation after we release lock on the BufMapping table.
4864		 */
4865		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
4866
4867		if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rlocator) &&
4868			BufTagGetForkNum(&bufHdr->tag) == forkNum &&
4869			bufHdr->tag.blockNum >= firstDelBlock)
4870			InvalidateBuffer(bufHdr);	/* releases spinlock */
4871		else
4872			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
4873	}
4874}
4875
4876/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
4877 *		DropDatabaseBuffers
4878 *
4879 *		This function removes all the buffers in the buffer cache for a
4880 *		particular database.  Dirty pages are simply dropped, without
4881 *		bothering to write them out first.  This is used when we destroy a
4882 *		database, to avoid trying to flush data to disk when the directory
4883 *		tree no longer exists.  Implementation is pretty similar to
4884 *		DropRelationBuffers() which is for destroying just one relation.
4885 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
4886 */
4887void
4888DropDatabaseBuffers(Oid dbid)
4889{
4890	int			i;
4891
4892	/*
4893	 * We needn't consider local buffers, since by assumption the target
4894	 * database isn't our own.
4895	 */
4896
4897	for (i = 0; i < NBuffers; i++)
4898	{
4899		BufferDesc *bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(i);
4900		uint32		buf_state;
4901
4902		/*
4903		 * As in DropRelationBuffers, an unlocked precheck should be safe and
4904		 * saves some cycles.
4905		 */
4906		if (bufHdr->tag.dbOid != dbid)
4907			continue;
4908
4909		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
4910		if (bufHdr->tag.dbOid == dbid)
4911			InvalidateBuffer(bufHdr);	/* releases spinlock */
4912		else
4913			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
4914	}
4915}
4916
4917/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
4918 *		FlushRelationBuffers
4919 *
4920 *		This function writes all dirty pages of a relation out to disk
4921 *		(or more accurately, out to kernel disk buffers), ensuring that the
4922 *		kernel has an up-to-date view of the relation.
4923 *
4924 *		Generally, the caller should be holding AccessExclusiveLock on the
4925 *		target relation to ensure that no other backend is busy dirtying
4926 *		more blocks of the relation; the effects can't be expected to last
4927 *		after the lock is released.
4928 *
4929 *		XXX currently it sequentially searches the buffer pool, should be
4930 *		changed to more clever ways of searching.  This routine is not
4931 *		used in any performance-critical code paths, so it's not worth
4932 *		adding additional overhead to normal paths to make it go faster.
4933 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
4934 */
4935void
4936FlushRelationBuffers(Relation rel)
4937{
4938	int			i;
4939	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
4940	SMgrRelation srel = RelationGetSmgr(rel);
4941
4942	if (RelationUsesLocalBuffers(rel))
4943	{
4944		for (i = 0; i < NLocBuffer; i++)
4945		{
4946			uint32		buf_state;
4947
4948			bufHdr = GetLocalBufferDescriptor(i);
4949			if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rel->rd_locator) &&
4950				((buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&bufHdr->state)) &
4951				 (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY)) == (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY))
4952			{
4953				ErrorContextCallback errcallback;
4954
4955				/* Setup error traceback support for ereport() */
4956				errcallback.callback = local_buffer_write_error_callback;
4957				errcallback.arg = bufHdr;
4958				errcallback.previous = error_context_stack;
4959				error_context_stack = &errcallback;
4960
4961				/* Make sure we can handle the pin */
4962				ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
4963				ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
4964
4965				/*
4966				 * Pin/unpin mostly to make valgrind work, but it also seems
4967				 * like the right thing to do.
4968				 */
4969				PinLocalBuffer(bufHdr, false);
4970
4971
4972				FlushLocalBuffer(bufHdr, srel);
4973
4974				UnpinLocalBuffer(BufferDescriptorGetBuffer(bufHdr));
4975
4976				/* Pop the error context stack */
4977				error_context_stack = errcallback.previous;
4978			}
4979		}
4980
4981		return;
4982	}
4983
4984	for (i = 0; i < NBuffers; i++)
4985	{
4986		uint32		buf_state;
4987
4988		bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(i);
4989
4990		/*
4991		 * As in DropRelationBuffers, an unlocked precheck should be safe and
4992		 * saves some cycles.
4993		 */
4994		if (!BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rel->rd_locator))
4995			continue;
4996
4997		/* Make sure we can handle the pin */
4998		ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
4999		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
5000
5001		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5002		if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &rel->rd_locator) &&
5003			(buf_state & (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY)) == (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY))
5004		{
5005			PinBuffer_Locked(bufHdr);
5006			LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr), LW_SHARED);
5007			FlushBuffer(bufHdr, srel, IOOBJECT_RELATION, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL);
5008			LWLockRelease(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr));
5009			UnpinBuffer(bufHdr);
5010		}
5011		else
5012			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5013	}
5014}
5015
5016/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
5017 *		FlushRelationsAllBuffers
5018 *
5019 *		This function flushes out of the buffer pool all the pages of all
5020 *		forks of the specified smgr relations.  It's equivalent to calling
5021 *		FlushRelationBuffers once per relation.  The relations are assumed not
5022 *		to use local buffers.
5023 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
5024 */
5025void
5026FlushRelationsAllBuffers(SMgrRelation *smgrs, int nrels)
5027{
5028	int			i;
5029	SMgrSortArray *srels;
5030	bool		use_bsearch;
5031
5032	if (nrels == 0)
5033		return;
5034
5035	/* fill-in array for qsort */
5036	srels = palloc(sizeof(SMgrSortArray) * nrels);
5037
5038	for (i = 0; i < nrels; i++)
5039	{
5040		Assert(!RelFileLocatorBackendIsTemp(smgrs[i]->smgr_rlocator));
5041
5042		srels[i].rlocator = smgrs[i]->smgr_rlocator.locator;
5043		srels[i].srel = smgrs[i];
5044	}
5045
5046	/*
5047	 * Save the bsearch overhead for low number of relations to sync. See
5048	 * DropRelationsAllBuffers for details.
5049	 */
5050	use_bsearch = nrels > RELS_BSEARCH_THRESHOLD;
5051
5052	/* sort the list of SMgrRelations if necessary */
5053	if (use_bsearch)
5054		qsort(srels, nrels, sizeof(SMgrSortArray), rlocator_comparator);
5055
5056	for (i = 0; i < NBuffers; i++)
5057	{
5058		SMgrSortArray *srelent = NULL;
5059		BufferDesc *bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(i);
5060		uint32		buf_state;
5061
5062		/*
5063		 * As in DropRelationBuffers, an unlocked precheck should be safe and
5064		 * saves some cycles.
5065		 */
5066
5067		if (!use_bsearch)
5068		{
5069			int			j;
5070
5071			for (j = 0; j < nrels; j++)
5072			{
5073				if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &srels[j].rlocator))
5074				{
5075					srelent = &srels[j];
5076					break;
5077				}
5078			}
5079		}
5080		else
5081		{
5082			RelFileLocator rlocator;
5083
5084			rlocator = BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag);
5085			srelent = bsearch(&rlocator,
5086							  srels, nrels, sizeof(SMgrSortArray),
5087							  rlocator_comparator);
5088		}
5089
5090		/* buffer doesn't belong to any of the given relfilelocators; skip it */
5091		if (srelent == NULL)
5092			continue;
5093
5094		/* Make sure we can handle the pin */
5095		ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
5096		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
5097
5098		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5099		if (BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag, &srelent->rlocator) &&
5100			(buf_state & (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY)) == (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY))
5101		{
5102			PinBuffer_Locked(bufHdr);
5103			LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr), LW_SHARED);
5104			FlushBuffer(bufHdr, srelent->srel, IOOBJECT_RELATION, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL);
5105			LWLockRelease(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr));
5106			UnpinBuffer(bufHdr);
5107		}
5108		else
5109			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5110	}
5111
5112	pfree(srels);
5113}
5114
5115/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
5116 *		RelationCopyStorageUsingBuffer
5117 *
5118 *		Copy fork's data using bufmgr.  Same as RelationCopyStorage but instead
5119 *		of using smgrread and smgrextend this will copy using bufmgr APIs.
5120 *
5121 *		Refer comments atop CreateAndCopyRelationData() for details about
5122 *		'permanent' parameter.
5123 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
5124 */
5125static void
5126RelationCopyStorageUsingBuffer(RelFileLocator srclocator,
5127							   RelFileLocator dstlocator,
5128							   ForkNumber forkNum, bool permanent)
5129{
5130	Buffer		srcBuf;
5131	Buffer		dstBuf;
5132	Page		srcPage;
5133	Page		dstPage;
5134	bool		use_wal;
5135	BlockNumber nblocks;
5136	BlockNumber blkno;
5137	PGIOAlignedBlock buf;
5138	BufferAccessStrategy bstrategy_src;
5139	BufferAccessStrategy bstrategy_dst;
5140	BlockRangeReadStreamPrivate p;
5141	ReadStream *src_stream;
5142	SMgrRelation src_smgr;
5143
5144	/*
5145	 * In general, we want to write WAL whenever wal_level > 'minimal', but we
5146	 * can skip it when copying any fork of an unlogged relation other than
5147	 * the init fork.
5148	 */
5149	use_wal = XLogIsNeeded() && (permanent || forkNum == INIT_FORKNUM);
5150
5151	/* Get number of blocks in the source relation. */
5152	nblocks = smgrnblocks(smgropen(srclocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER),
5153						  forkNum);
5154
5155	/* Nothing to copy; just return. */
5156	if (nblocks == 0)
5157		return;
5158
5159	/*
5160	 * Bulk extend the destination relation of the same size as the source
5161	 * relation before starting to copy block by block.
5162	 */
5163	memset(buf.data, 0, BLCKSZ);
5164	smgrextend(smgropen(dstlocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER), forkNum, nblocks - 1,
5165			   buf.data, true);
5166
5167	/* This is a bulk operation, so use buffer access strategies. */
5168	bstrategy_src = GetAccessStrategy(BAS_BULKREAD);
5169	bstrategy_dst = GetAccessStrategy(BAS_BULKWRITE);
5170
5171	/* Initialize streaming read */
5172	p.current_blocknum = 0;
5173	p.last_exclusive = nblocks;
5174	src_smgr = smgropen(srclocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER);
5175
5176	/*
5177	 * It is safe to use batchmode as block_range_read_stream_cb takes no
5178	 * locks.
5179	 */
5180	src_stream = read_stream_begin_smgr_relation(READ_STREAM_FULL |
5181												 READ_STREAM_USE_BATCHING,
5182												 bstrategy_src,
5183												 src_smgr,
5184												 permanent ? RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT : RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED,
5185												 forkNum,
5186												 block_range_read_stream_cb,
5187												 &p,
5188												 0);
5189
5190	/* Iterate over each block of the source relation file. */
5191	for (blkno = 0; blkno < nblocks; blkno++)
5192	{
5193		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
5194
5195		/* Read block from source relation. */
5196		srcBuf = read_stream_next_buffer(src_stream, NULL);
5197		LockBuffer(srcBuf, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
5198		srcPage = BufferGetPage(srcBuf);
5199
5200		dstBuf = ReadBufferWithoutRelcache(dstlocator, forkNum,
5201										   BufferGetBlockNumber(srcBuf),
5202										   RBM_ZERO_AND_LOCK, bstrategy_dst,
5203										   permanent);
5204		dstPage = BufferGetPage(dstBuf);
5205
5206		START_CRIT_SECTION();
5207
5208		/* Copy page data from the source to the destination. */
5209		memcpy(dstPage, srcPage, BLCKSZ);
5210		MarkBufferDirty(dstBuf);
5211
5212		/* WAL-log the copied page. */
5213		if (use_wal)
5214			log_newpage_buffer(dstBuf, true);
5215
5216		END_CRIT_SECTION();
5217
5218		UnlockReleaseBuffer(dstBuf);
5219		UnlockReleaseBuffer(srcBuf);
5220	}
5221	Assert(read_stream_next_buffer(src_stream, NULL) == InvalidBuffer);
5222	read_stream_end(src_stream);
5223
5224	FreeAccessStrategy(bstrategy_src);
5225	FreeAccessStrategy(bstrategy_dst);
5226}
5227
5228/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
5229 *		CreateAndCopyRelationData
5230 *
5231 *		Create destination relation storage and copy all forks from the
5232 *		source relation to the destination.
5233 *
5234 *		Pass permanent as true for permanent relations and false for
5235 *		unlogged relations.  Currently this API is not supported for
5236 *		temporary relations.
5237 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
5238 */
5239void
5240CreateAndCopyRelationData(RelFileLocator src_rlocator,
5241						  RelFileLocator dst_rlocator, bool permanent)
5242{
5243	char		relpersistence;
5244	SMgrRelation src_rel;
5245	SMgrRelation dst_rel;
5246
5247	/* Set the relpersistence. */
5248	relpersistence = permanent ?
5249		RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT : RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED;
5250
5251	src_rel = smgropen(src_rlocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER);
5252	dst_rel = smgropen(dst_rlocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER);
5253
5254	/*
5255	 * Create and copy all forks of the relation.  During create database we
5256	 * have a separate cleanup mechanism which deletes complete database
5257	 * directory.  Therefore, each individual relation doesn't need to be
5258	 * registered for cleanup.
5259	 */
5260	RelationCreateStorage(dst_rlocator, relpersistence, false);
5261
5262	/* copy main fork. */
5263	RelationCopyStorageUsingBuffer(src_rlocator, dst_rlocator, MAIN_FORKNUM,
5264								   permanent);
5265
5266	/* copy those extra forks that exist */
5267	for (ForkNumber forkNum = MAIN_FORKNUM + 1;
5268		 forkNum <= MAX_FORKNUM; forkNum++)
5269	{
5270		if (smgrexists(src_rel, forkNum))
5271		{
5272			smgrcreate(dst_rel, forkNum, false);
5273
5274			/*
5275			 * WAL log creation if the relation is persistent, or this is the
5276			 * init fork of an unlogged relation.
5277			 */
5278			if (permanent || forkNum == INIT_FORKNUM)
5279				log_smgrcreate(&dst_rlocator, forkNum);
5280
5281			/* Copy a fork's data, block by block. */
5282			RelationCopyStorageUsingBuffer(src_rlocator, dst_rlocator, forkNum,
5283										   permanent);
5284		}
5285	}
5286}
5287
5288/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
5289 *		FlushDatabaseBuffers
5290 *
5291 *		This function writes all dirty pages of a database out to disk
5292 *		(or more accurately, out to kernel disk buffers), ensuring that the
5293 *		kernel has an up-to-date view of the database.
5294 *
5295 *		Generally, the caller should be holding an appropriate lock to ensure
5296 *		no other backend is active in the target database; otherwise more
5297 *		pages could get dirtied.
5298 *
5299 *		Note we don't worry about flushing any pages of temporary relations.
5300 *		It's assumed these wouldn't be interesting.
5301 * --------------------------------------------------------------------
5302 */
5303void
5304FlushDatabaseBuffers(Oid dbid)
5305{
5306	int			i;
5307	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5308
5309	for (i = 0; i < NBuffers; i++)
5310	{
5311		uint32		buf_state;
5312
5313		bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(i);
5314
5315		/*
5316		 * As in DropRelationBuffers, an unlocked precheck should be safe and
5317		 * saves some cycles.
5318		 */
5319		if (bufHdr->tag.dbOid != dbid)
5320			continue;
5321
5322		/* Make sure we can handle the pin */
5323		ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
5324		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
5325
5326		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5327		if (bufHdr->tag.dbOid == dbid &&
5328			(buf_state & (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY)) == (BM_VALID | BM_DIRTY))
5329		{
5330			PinBuffer_Locked(bufHdr);
5331			LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr), LW_SHARED);
5332			FlushBuffer(bufHdr, NULL, IOOBJECT_RELATION, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL);
5333			LWLockRelease(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr));
5334			UnpinBuffer(bufHdr);
5335		}
5336		else
5337			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5338	}
5339}
5340
5341/*
5342 * Flush a previously, shared or exclusively, locked and pinned buffer to the
5343 * OS.
5344 */
5345void
5346FlushOneBuffer(Buffer buffer)
5347{
5348	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5349
5350	/* currently not needed, but no fundamental reason not to support */
5351	Assert(!BufferIsLocal(buffer));
5352
5353	Assert(BufferIsPinned(buffer));
5354
5355	bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5356
5357	Assert(LWLockHeldByMe(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr)));
5358
5359	FlushBuffer(bufHdr, NULL, IOOBJECT_RELATION, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL);
5360}
5361
5362/*
5363 * ReleaseBuffer -- release the pin on a buffer
5364 */
5365void
5366ReleaseBuffer(Buffer buffer)
5367{
5368	if (!BufferIsValid(buffer))
5369		elog(ERROR, "bad buffer ID: %d", buffer);
5370
5371	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5372		UnpinLocalBuffer(buffer);
5373	else
5374		UnpinBuffer(GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1));
5375}
5376
5377/*
5378 * UnlockReleaseBuffer -- release the content lock and pin on a buffer
5379 *
5380 * This is just a shorthand for a common combination.
5381 */
5382void
5383UnlockReleaseBuffer(Buffer buffer)
5384{
5385	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
5386	ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
5387}
5388
5389/*
5390 * IncrBufferRefCount
5391 *		Increment the pin count on a buffer that we have *already* pinned
5392 *		at least once.
5393 *
5394 *		This function cannot be used on a buffer we do not have pinned,
5395 *		because it doesn't change the shared buffer state.
5396 */
5397void
5398IncrBufferRefCount(Buffer buffer)
5399{
5400	Assert(BufferIsPinned(buffer));
5401	ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
5402	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5403		LocalRefCount[-buffer - 1]++;
5404	else
5405	{
5406		PrivateRefCountEntry *ref;
5407
5408		ref = GetPrivateRefCountEntry(buffer, true);
5409		Assert(ref != NULL);
5410		ref->refcount++;
5411	}
5412	ResourceOwnerRememberBuffer(CurrentResourceOwner, buffer);
5413}
5414
5415/*
5416 * MarkBufferDirtyHint
5417 *
5418 *	Mark a buffer dirty for non-critical changes.
5419 *
5420 * This is essentially the same as MarkBufferDirty, except:
5421 *
5422 * 1. The caller does not write WAL; so if checksums are enabled, we may need
5423 *	  to write an XLOG_FPI_FOR_HINT WAL record to protect against torn pages.
5424 * 2. The caller might have only share-lock instead of exclusive-lock on the
5425 *	  buffer's content lock.
5426 * 3. This function does not guarantee that the buffer is always marked dirty
5427 *	  (due to a race condition), so it cannot be used for important changes.
5428 */
5429void
5430MarkBufferDirtyHint(Buffer buffer, bool buffer_std)
5431{
5432	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5433	Page		page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
5434
5435	if (!BufferIsValid(buffer))
5436		elog(ERROR, "bad buffer ID: %d", buffer);
5437
5438	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5439	{
5440		MarkLocalBufferDirty(buffer);
5441		return;
5442	}
5443
5444	bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5445
5446	Assert(GetPrivateRefCount(buffer) > 0);
5447	/* here, either share or exclusive lock is OK */
5448	Assert(LWLockHeldByMe(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr)));
5449
5450	/*
5451	 * This routine might get called many times on the same page, if we are
5452	 * making the first scan after commit of an xact that added/deleted many
5453	 * tuples. So, be as quick as we can if the buffer is already dirty.  We
5454	 * do this by not acquiring spinlock if it looks like the status bits are
5455	 * already set.  Since we make this test unlocked, there's a chance we
5456	 * might fail to notice that the flags have just been cleared, and failed
5457	 * to reset them, due to memory-ordering issues.  But since this function
5458	 * is only intended to be used in cases where failing to write out the
5459	 * data would be harmless anyway, it doesn't really matter.
5460	 */
5461	if ((pg_atomic_read_u32(&bufHdr->state) & (BM_DIRTY | BM_JUST_DIRTIED)) !=
5462		(BM_DIRTY | BM_JUST_DIRTIED))
5463	{
5464		XLogRecPtr	lsn = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
5465		bool		dirtied = false;
5466		bool		delayChkptFlags = false;
5467		uint32		buf_state;
5468
5469		/*
5470		 * If we need to protect hint bit updates from torn writes, WAL-log a
5471		 * full page image of the page. This full page image is only necessary
5472		 * if the hint bit update is the first change to the page since the
5473		 * last checkpoint.
5474		 *
5475		 * We don't check full_page_writes here because that logic is included
5476		 * when we call XLogInsert() since the value changes dynamically.
5477		 */
5478		if (XLogHintBitIsNeeded() &&
5479			(pg_atomic_read_u32(&bufHdr->state) & BM_PERMANENT))
5480		{
5481			/*
5482			 * If we must not write WAL, due to a relfilelocator-specific
5483			 * condition or being in recovery, don't dirty the page.  We can
5484			 * set the hint, just not dirty the page as a result so the hint
5485			 * is lost when we evict the page or shutdown.
5486			 *
5487			 * See src/backend/storage/page/README for longer discussion.
5488			 */
5489			if (RecoveryInProgress() ||
5490				RelFileLocatorSkippingWAL(BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag)))
5491				return;
5492
5493			/*
5494			 * If the block is already dirty because we either made a change
5495			 * or set a hint already, then we don't need to write a full page
5496			 * image.  Note that aggressive cleaning of blocks dirtied by hint
5497			 * bit setting would increase the call rate. Bulk setting of hint
5498			 * bits would reduce the call rate...
5499			 *
5500			 * We must issue the WAL record before we mark the buffer dirty.
5501			 * Otherwise we might write the page before we write the WAL. That
5502			 * causes a race condition, since a checkpoint might occur between
5503			 * writing the WAL record and marking the buffer dirty. We solve
5504			 * that with a kluge, but one that is already in use during
5505			 * transaction commit to prevent race conditions. Basically, we
5506			 * simply prevent the checkpoint WAL record from being written
5507			 * until we have marked the buffer dirty. We don't start the
5508			 * checkpoint flush until we have marked dirty, so our checkpoint
5509			 * must flush the change to disk successfully or the checkpoint
5510			 * never gets written, so crash recovery will fix.
5511			 *
5512			 * It's possible we may enter here without an xid, so it is
5513			 * essential that CreateCheckPoint waits for virtual transactions
5514			 * rather than full transactionids.
5515			 */
5516			Assert((MyProc->delayChkptFlags & DELAY_CHKPT_START) == 0);
5517			MyProc->delayChkptFlags |= DELAY_CHKPT_START;
5518			delayChkptFlags = true;
5519			lsn = XLogSaveBufferForHint(buffer, buffer_std);
5520		}
5521
5522		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5523
5524		Assert(BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) > 0);
5525
5526		if (!(buf_state & BM_DIRTY))
5527		{
5528			dirtied = true;		/* Means "will be dirtied by this action" */
5529
5530			/*
5531			 * Set the page LSN if we wrote a backup block. We aren't supposed
5532			 * to set this when only holding a share lock but as long as we
5533			 * serialise it somehow we're OK. We choose to set LSN while
5534			 * holding the buffer header lock, which causes any reader of an
5535			 * LSN who holds only a share lock to also obtain a buffer header
5536			 * lock before using PageGetLSN(), which is enforced in
5537			 * BufferGetLSNAtomic().
5538			 *
5539			 * If checksums are enabled, you might think we should reset the
5540			 * checksum here. That will happen when the page is written
5541			 * sometime later in this checkpoint cycle.
5542			 */
5543			if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(lsn))
5544				PageSetLSN(page, lsn);
5545		}
5546
5547		buf_state |= BM_DIRTY | BM_JUST_DIRTIED;
5548		UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5549
5550		if (delayChkptFlags)
5551			MyProc->delayChkptFlags &= ~DELAY_CHKPT_START;
5552
5553		if (dirtied)
5554		{
5555			pgBufferUsage.shared_blks_dirtied++;
5556			if (VacuumCostActive)
5557				VacuumCostBalance += VacuumCostPageDirty;
5558		}
5559	}
5560}
5561
5562/*
5563 * Release buffer content locks for shared buffers.
5564 *
5565 * Used to clean up after errors.
5566 *
5567 * Currently, we can expect that lwlock.c's LWLockReleaseAll() took care
5568 * of releasing buffer content locks per se; the only thing we need to deal
5569 * with here is clearing any PIN_COUNT request that was in progress.
5570 */
5571void
5572UnlockBuffers(void)
5573{
5574	BufferDesc *buf = PinCountWaitBuf;
5575
5576	if (buf)
5577	{
5578		uint32		buf_state;
5579
5580		buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf);
5581
5582		/*
5583		 * Don't complain if flag bit not set; it could have been reset but we
5584		 * got a cancel/die interrupt before getting the signal.
5585		 */
5586		if ((buf_state & BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER) != 0 &&
5587			buf->wait_backend_pgprocno == MyProcNumber)
5588			buf_state &= ~BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER;
5589
5590		UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
5591
5592		PinCountWaitBuf = NULL;
5593	}
5594}
5595
5596/*
5597 * Acquire or release the content_lock for the buffer.
5598 */
5599void
5600LockBuffer(Buffer buffer, int mode)
5601{
5602	BufferDesc *buf;
5603
5604	Assert(BufferIsPinned(buffer));
5605	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5606		return;					/* local buffers need no lock */
5607
5608	buf = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5609
5610	if (mode == BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK)
5611		LWLockRelease(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(buf));
5612	else if (mode == BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE)
5613		LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(buf), LW_SHARED);
5614	else if (mode == BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE)
5615		LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(buf), LW_EXCLUSIVE);
5616	else
5617		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized buffer lock mode: %d", mode);
5618}
5619
5620/*
5621 * Acquire the content_lock for the buffer, but only if we don't have to wait.
5622 *
5623 * This assumes the caller wants BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE mode.
5624 */
5625bool
5626ConditionalLockBuffer(Buffer buffer)
5627{
5628	BufferDesc *buf;
5629
5630	Assert(BufferIsPinned(buffer));
5631	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5632		return true;			/* act as though we got it */
5633
5634	buf = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5635
5636	return LWLockConditionalAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(buf),
5637									LW_EXCLUSIVE);
5638}
5639
5640/*
5641 * Verify that this backend is pinning the buffer exactly once.
5642 *
5643 * NOTE: Like in BufferIsPinned(), what we check here is that *this* backend
5644 * holds a pin on the buffer.  We do not care whether some other backend does.
5645 */
5646void
5647CheckBufferIsPinnedOnce(Buffer buffer)
5648{
5649	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5650	{
5651		if (LocalRefCount[-buffer - 1] != 1)
5652			elog(ERROR, "incorrect local pin count: %d",
5653				 LocalRefCount[-buffer - 1]);
5654	}
5655	else
5656	{
5657		if (GetPrivateRefCount(buffer) != 1)
5658			elog(ERROR, "incorrect local pin count: %d",
5659				 GetPrivateRefCount(buffer));
5660	}
5661}
5662
5663/*
5664 * LockBufferForCleanup - lock a buffer in preparation for deleting items
5665 *
5666 * Items may be deleted from a disk page only when the caller (a) holds an
5667 * exclusive lock on the buffer and (b) has observed that no other backend
5668 * holds a pin on the buffer.  If there is a pin, then the other backend
5669 * might have a pointer into the buffer (for example, a heapscan reference
5670 * to an item --- see README for more details).  It's OK if a pin is added
5671 * after the cleanup starts, however; the newly-arrived backend will be
5672 * unable to look at the page until we release the exclusive lock.
5673 *
5674 * To implement this protocol, a would-be deleter must pin the buffer and
5675 * then call LockBufferForCleanup().  LockBufferForCleanup() is similar to
5676 * LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE), except that it loops until
5677 * it has successfully observed pin count = 1.
5678 */
5679void
5680LockBufferForCleanup(Buffer buffer)
5681{
5682	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5683	TimestampTz waitStart = 0;
5684	bool		waiting = false;
5685	bool		logged_recovery_conflict = false;
5686
5687	Assert(BufferIsPinned(buffer));
5688	Assert(PinCountWaitBuf == NULL);
5689
5690	CheckBufferIsPinnedOnce(buffer);
5691
5692	/*
5693	 * We do not yet need to be worried about in-progress AIOs holding a pin,
5694	 * as we, so far, only support doing reads via AIO and this function can
5695	 * only be called once the buffer is valid (i.e. no read can be in
5696	 * flight).
5697	 */
5698
5699	/* Nobody else to wait for */
5700	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5701		return;
5702
5703	bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5704
5705	for (;;)
5706	{
5707		uint32		buf_state;
5708
5709		/* Try to acquire lock */
5710		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
5711		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5712
5713		Assert(BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) > 0);
5714		if (BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) == 1)
5715		{
5716			/* Successfully acquired exclusive lock with pincount 1 */
5717			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5718
5719			/*
5720			 * Emit the log message if recovery conflict on buffer pin was
5721			 * resolved but the startup process waited longer than
5722			 * deadlock_timeout for it.
5723			 */
5724			if (logged_recovery_conflict)
5725				LogRecoveryConflict(PROCSIG_RECOVERY_CONFLICT_BUFFERPIN,
5726									waitStart, GetCurrentTimestamp(),
5727									NULL, false);
5728
5729			if (waiting)
5730			{
5731				/* reset ps display to remove the suffix if we added one */
5732				set_ps_display_remove_suffix();
5733				waiting = false;
5734			}
5735			return;
5736		}
5737		/* Failed, so mark myself as waiting for pincount 1 */
5738		if (buf_state & BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER)
5739		{
5740			UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5741			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
5742			elog(ERROR, "multiple backends attempting to wait for pincount 1");
5743		}
5744		bufHdr->wait_backend_pgprocno = MyProcNumber;
5745		PinCountWaitBuf = bufHdr;
5746		buf_state |= BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER;
5747		UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5748		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
5749
5750		/* Wait to be signaled by UnpinBuffer() */
5751		if (InHotStandby)
5752		{
5753			if (!waiting)
5754			{
5755				/* adjust the process title to indicate that it's waiting */
5756				set_ps_display_suffix("waiting");
5757				waiting = true;
5758			}
5759
5760			/*
5761			 * Emit the log message if the startup process is waiting longer
5762			 * than deadlock_timeout for recovery conflict on buffer pin.
5763			 *
5764			 * Skip this if first time through because the startup process has
5765			 * not started waiting yet in this case. So, the wait start
5766			 * timestamp is set after this logic.
5767			 */
5768			if (waitStart != 0 && !logged_recovery_conflict)
5769			{
5770				TimestampTz now = GetCurrentTimestamp();
5771
5772				if (TimestampDifferenceExceeds(waitStart, now,
5773											   DeadlockTimeout))
5774				{
5775					LogRecoveryConflict(PROCSIG_RECOVERY_CONFLICT_BUFFERPIN,
5776										waitStart, now, NULL, true);
5777					logged_recovery_conflict = true;
5778				}
5779			}
5780
5781			/*
5782			 * Set the wait start timestamp if logging is enabled and first
5783			 * time through.
5784			 */
5785			if (log_recovery_conflict_waits && waitStart == 0)
5786				waitStart = GetCurrentTimestamp();
5787
5788			/* Publish the bufid that Startup process waits on */
5789			SetStartupBufferPinWaitBufId(buffer - 1);
5790			/* Set alarm and then wait to be signaled by UnpinBuffer() */
5791			ResolveRecoveryConflictWithBufferPin();
5792			/* Reset the published bufid */
5793			SetStartupBufferPinWaitBufId(-1);
5794		}
5795		else
5796			ProcWaitForSignal(WAIT_EVENT_BUFFER_PIN);
5797
5798		/*
5799		 * Remove flag marking us as waiter. Normally this will not be set
5800		 * anymore, but ProcWaitForSignal() can return for other signals as
5801		 * well.  We take care to only reset the flag if we're the waiter, as
5802		 * theoretically another backend could have started waiting. That's
5803		 * impossible with the current usages due to table level locking, but
5804		 * better be safe.
5805		 */
5806		buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5807		if ((buf_state & BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER) != 0 &&
5808			bufHdr->wait_backend_pgprocno == MyProcNumber)
5809			buf_state &= ~BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER;
5810		UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5811
5812		PinCountWaitBuf = NULL;
5813		/* Loop back and try again */
5814	}
5815}
5816
5817/*
5818 * Check called from ProcessRecoveryConflictInterrupts() when Startup process
5819 * requests cancellation of all pin holders that are blocking it.
5820 */
5821bool
5822HoldingBufferPinThatDelaysRecovery(void)
5823{
5824	int			bufid = GetStartupBufferPinWaitBufId();
5825
5826	/*
5827	 * If we get woken slowly then it's possible that the Startup process was
5828	 * already woken by other backends before we got here. Also possible that
5829	 * we get here by multiple interrupts or interrupts at inappropriate
5830	 * times, so make sure we do nothing if the bufid is not set.
5831	 */
5832	if (bufid < 0)
5833		return false;
5834
5835	if (GetPrivateRefCount(bufid + 1) > 0)
5836		return true;
5837
5838	return false;
5839}
5840
5841/*
5842 * ConditionalLockBufferForCleanup - as above, but don't wait to get the lock
5843 *
5844 * We won't loop, but just check once to see if the pin count is OK.  If
5845 * not, return false with no lock held.
5846 */
5847bool
5848ConditionalLockBufferForCleanup(Buffer buffer)
5849{
5850	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5851	uint32		buf_state,
5852				refcount;
5853
5854	Assert(BufferIsValid(buffer));
5855
5856	/* see AIO related comment in LockBufferForCleanup() */
5857
5858	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5859	{
5860		refcount = LocalRefCount[-buffer - 1];
5861		/* There should be exactly one pin */
5862		Assert(refcount > 0);
5863		if (refcount != 1)
5864			return false;
5865		/* Nobody else to wait for */
5866		return true;
5867	}
5868
5869	/* There should be exactly one local pin */
5870	refcount = GetPrivateRefCount(buffer);
5871	Assert(refcount);
5872	if (refcount != 1)
5873		return false;
5874
5875	/* Try to acquire lock */
5876	if (!ConditionalLockBuffer(buffer))
5877		return false;
5878
5879	bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5880	buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5881	refcount = BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state);
5882
5883	Assert(refcount > 0);
5884	if (refcount == 1)
5885	{
5886		/* Successfully acquired exclusive lock with pincount 1 */
5887		UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5888		return true;
5889	}
5890
5891	/* Failed, so release the lock */
5892	UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5893	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
5894	return false;
5895}
5896
5897/*
5898 * IsBufferCleanupOK - as above, but we already have the lock
5899 *
5900 * Check whether it's OK to perform cleanup on a buffer we've already
5901 * locked.  If we observe that the pin count is 1, our exclusive lock
5902 * happens to be a cleanup lock, and we can proceed with anything that
5903 * would have been allowable had we sought a cleanup lock originally.
5904 */
5905bool
5906IsBufferCleanupOK(Buffer buffer)
5907{
5908	BufferDesc *bufHdr;
5909	uint32		buf_state;
5910
5911	Assert(BufferIsValid(buffer));
5912
5913	/* see AIO related comment in LockBufferForCleanup() */
5914
5915	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
5916	{
5917		/* There should be exactly one pin */
5918		if (LocalRefCount[-buffer - 1] != 1)
5919			return false;
5920		/* Nobody else to wait for */
5921		return true;
5922	}
5923
5924	/* There should be exactly one local pin */
5925	if (GetPrivateRefCount(buffer) != 1)
5926		return false;
5927
5928	bufHdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
5929
5930	/* caller must hold exclusive lock on buffer */
5931	Assert(LWLockHeldByMeInMode(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(bufHdr),
5932								LW_EXCLUSIVE));
5933
5934	buf_state = LockBufHdr(bufHdr);
5935
5936	Assert(BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) > 0);
5937	if (BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) == 1)
5938	{
5939		/* pincount is OK. */
5940		UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5941		return true;
5942	}
5943
5944	UnlockBufHdr(bufHdr, buf_state);
5945	return false;
5946}
5947
5948
5949/*
5950 *	Functions for buffer I/O handling
5951 *
5952 *	Also note that these are used only for shared buffers, not local ones.
5953 */
5954
5955/*
5956 * WaitIO -- Block until the IO_IN_PROGRESS flag on 'buf' is cleared.
5957 */
5958static void
5959WaitIO(BufferDesc *buf)
5960{
5961	ConditionVariable *cv = BufferDescriptorGetIOCV(buf);
5962
5963	ConditionVariablePrepareToSleep(cv);
5964	for (;;)
5965	{
5966		uint32		buf_state;
5967		PgAioWaitRef iow;
5968
5969		/*
5970		 * It may not be necessary to acquire the spinlock to check the flag
5971		 * here, but since this test is essential for correctness, we'd better
5972		 * play it safe.
5973		 */
5974		buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf);
5975
5976		/*
5977		 * Copy the wait reference while holding the spinlock. This protects
5978		 * against a concurrent TerminateBufferIO() in another backend from
5979		 * clearing the wref while it's being read.
5980		 */
5981		iow = buf->io_wref;
5982		UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
5983
5984		/* no IO in progress, we don't need to wait */
5985		if (!(buf_state & BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS))
5986			break;
5987
5988		/*
5989		 * The buffer has asynchronous IO in progress, wait for it to
5990		 * complete.
5991		 */
5992		if (pgaio_wref_valid(&iow))
5993		{
5994			pgaio_wref_wait(&iow);
5995
5996			/*
5997			 * The AIO subsystem internally uses condition variables and thus
5998			 * might remove this backend from the BufferDesc's CV. While that
5999			 * wouldn't cause a correctness issue (the first CV sleep just
6000			 * immediately returns if not already registered), it seems worth
6001			 * avoiding unnecessary loop iterations, given that we take care
6002			 * to do so at the start of the function.
6003			 */
6004			ConditionVariablePrepareToSleep(cv);
6005			continue;
6006		}
6007
6008		/* wait on BufferDesc->cv, e.g. for concurrent synchronous IO */
6009		ConditionVariableSleep(cv, WAIT_EVENT_BUFFER_IO);
6010	}
6011	ConditionVariableCancelSleep();
6012}
6013
6014/*
6015 * StartBufferIO: begin I/O on this buffer
6016 *	(Assumptions)
6017 *	My process is executing no IO on this buffer
6018 *	The buffer is Pinned
6019 *
6020 * In some scenarios multiple backends could attempt the same I/O operation
6021 * concurrently.  If someone else has already started I/O on this buffer then
6022 * we will wait for completion of the IO using WaitIO().
6023 *
6024 * Input operations are only attempted on buffers that are not BM_VALID,
6025 * and output operations only on buffers that are BM_VALID and BM_DIRTY,
6026 * so we can always tell if the work is already done.
6027 *
6028 * Returns true if we successfully marked the buffer as I/O busy,
6029 * false if someone else already did the work.
6030 *
6031 * If nowait is true, then we don't wait for an I/O to be finished by another
6032 * backend.  In that case, false indicates either that the I/O was already
6033 * finished, or is still in progress.  This is useful for callers that want to
6034 * find out if they can perform the I/O as part of a larger operation, without
6035 * waiting for the answer or distinguishing the reasons why not.
6036 */
6037bool
6038StartBufferIO(BufferDesc *buf, bool forInput, bool nowait)
6039{
6040	uint32		buf_state;
6041
6042	ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
6043
6044	for (;;)
6045	{
6046		buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf);
6047
6048		if (!(buf_state & BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS))
6049			break;
6050		UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
6051		if (nowait)
6052			return false;
6053		WaitIO(buf);
6054	}
6055
6056	/* Once we get here, there is definitely no I/O active on this buffer */
6057
6058	/* Check if someone else already did the I/O */
6059	if (forInput ? (buf_state & BM_VALID) : !(buf_state & BM_DIRTY))
6060	{
6061		UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
6062		return false;
6063	}
6064
6065	buf_state |= BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS;
6066	UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
6067
6068	ResourceOwnerRememberBufferIO(CurrentResourceOwner,
6069								  BufferDescriptorGetBuffer(buf));
6070
6071	return true;
6072}
6073
6074/*
6075 * TerminateBufferIO: release a buffer we were doing I/O on
6076 *	(Assumptions)
6077 *	My process is executing IO for the buffer
6078 *	BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS bit is set for the buffer
6079 *	The buffer is Pinned
6080 *
6081 * If clear_dirty is true and BM_JUST_DIRTIED is not set, we clear the
6082 * buffer's BM_DIRTY flag.  This is appropriate when terminating a
6083 * successful write.  The check on BM_JUST_DIRTIED is necessary to avoid
6084 * marking the buffer clean if it was re-dirtied while we were writing.
6085 *
6086 * set_flag_bits gets ORed into the buffer's flags.  It must include
6087 * BM_IO_ERROR in a failure case.  For successful completion it could
6088 * be 0, or BM_VALID if we just finished reading in the page.
6089 *
6090 * If forget_owner is true, we release the buffer I/O from the current
6091 * resource owner. (forget_owner=false is used when the resource owner itself
6092 * is being released)
6093 */
6094void
6095TerminateBufferIO(BufferDesc *buf, bool clear_dirty, uint32 set_flag_bits,
6096				  bool forget_owner, bool release_aio)
6097{
6098	uint32		buf_state;
6099
6100	buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf);
6101
6102	Assert(buf_state & BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS);
6103	buf_state &= ~BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS;
6104
6105	/* Clear earlier errors, if this IO failed, it'll be marked again */
6106	buf_state &= ~BM_IO_ERROR;
6107
6108	if (clear_dirty && !(buf_state & BM_JUST_DIRTIED))
6109		buf_state &= ~(BM_DIRTY | BM_CHECKPOINT_NEEDED);
6110
6111	if (release_aio)
6112	{
6113		/* release ownership by the AIO subsystem */
6114		Assert(BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) > 0);
6115		buf_state -= BUF_REFCOUNT_ONE;
6116		pgaio_wref_clear(&buf->io_wref);
6117	}
6118
6119	buf_state |= set_flag_bits;
6120	UnlockBufHdr(buf, buf_state);
6121
6122	if (forget_owner)
6123		ResourceOwnerForgetBufferIO(CurrentResourceOwner,
6124									BufferDescriptorGetBuffer(buf));
6125
6126	ConditionVariableBroadcast(BufferDescriptorGetIOCV(buf));
6127
6128	/*
6129	 * Support LockBufferForCleanup()
6130	 *
6131	 * We may have just released the last pin other than the waiter's. In most
6132	 * cases, this backend holds another pin on the buffer. But, if, for
6133	 * example, this backend is completing an IO issued by another backend, it
6134	 * may be time to wake the waiter.
6135	 */
6136	if (release_aio && (buf_state & BM_PIN_COUNT_WAITER))
6137		WakePinCountWaiter(buf);
6138}
6139
6140/*
6141 * AbortBufferIO: Clean up active buffer I/O after an error.
6142 *
6143 *	All LWLocks we might have held have been released,
6144 *	but we haven't yet released buffer pins, so the buffer is still pinned.
6145 *
6146 *	If I/O was in progress, we always set BM_IO_ERROR, even though it's
6147 *	possible the error condition wasn't related to the I/O.
6148 *
6149 *  Note: this does not remove the buffer I/O from the resource owner.
6150 *  That's correct when we're releasing the whole resource owner, but
6151 *  beware if you use this in other contexts.
6152 */
6153static void
6154AbortBufferIO(Buffer buffer)
6155{
6156	BufferDesc *buf_hdr = GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
6157	uint32		buf_state;
6158
6159	buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf_hdr);
6160	Assert(buf_state & (BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS | BM_TAG_VALID));
6161
6162	if (!(buf_state & BM_VALID))
6163	{
6164		Assert(!(buf_state & BM_DIRTY));
6165		UnlockBufHdr(buf_hdr, buf_state);
6166	}
6167	else
6168	{
6169		Assert(buf_state & BM_DIRTY);
6170		UnlockBufHdr(buf_hdr, buf_state);
6171
6172		/* Issue notice if this is not the first failure... */
6173		if (buf_state & BM_IO_ERROR)
6174		{
6175			/* Buffer is pinned, so we can read tag without spinlock */
6176			ereport(WARNING,
6177					(errcode(ERRCODE_IO_ERROR),
6178					 errmsg("could not write block %u of %s",
6179							buf_hdr->tag.blockNum,
6180							relpathperm(BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&buf_hdr->tag),
6181										BufTagGetForkNum(&buf_hdr->tag)).str),
6182					 errdetail("Multiple failures --- write error might be permanent.")));
6183		}
6184	}
6185
6186	TerminateBufferIO(buf_hdr, false, BM_IO_ERROR, false, false);
6187}
6188
6189/*
6190 * Error context callback for errors occurring during shared buffer writes.
6191 */
6192static void
6193shared_buffer_write_error_callback(void *arg)
6194{
6195	BufferDesc *bufHdr = (BufferDesc *) arg;
6196
6197	/* Buffer is pinned, so we can read the tag without locking the spinlock */
6198	if (bufHdr != NULL)
6199		errcontext("writing block %u of relation \"%s\"",
6200				   bufHdr->tag.blockNum,
6201				   relpathperm(BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag),
6202							   BufTagGetForkNum(&bufHdr->tag)).str);
6203}
6204
6205/*
6206 * Error context callback for errors occurring during local buffer writes.
6207 */
6208static void
6209local_buffer_write_error_callback(void *arg)
6210{
6211	BufferDesc *bufHdr = (BufferDesc *) arg;
6212
6213	if (bufHdr != NULL)
6214		errcontext("writing block %u of relation \"%s\"",
6215				   bufHdr->tag.blockNum,
6216				   relpathbackend(BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&bufHdr->tag),
6217								  MyProcNumber,
6218								  BufTagGetForkNum(&bufHdr->tag)).str);
6219}
6220
6221/*
6222 * RelFileLocator qsort/bsearch comparator; see RelFileLocatorEquals.
6223 */
6224static int
6225rlocator_comparator(const void *p1, const void *p2)
6226{
6227	RelFileLocator n1 = *(const RelFileLocator *) p1;
6228	RelFileLocator n2 = *(const RelFileLocator *) p2;
6229
6230	if (n1.relNumber < n2.relNumber)
6231		return -1;
6232	else if (n1.relNumber > n2.relNumber)
6233		return 1;
6234
6235	if (n1.dbOid < n2.dbOid)
6236		return -1;
6237	else if (n1.dbOid > n2.dbOid)
6238		return 1;
6239
6240	if (n1.spcOid < n2.spcOid)
6241		return -1;
6242	else if (n1.spcOid > n2.spcOid)
6243		return 1;
6244	else
6245		return 0;
6246}
6247
6248/*
6249 * Lock buffer header - set BM_LOCKED in buffer state.
6250 */
6251uint32
6252LockBufHdr(BufferDesc *desc)
6253{
6254	SpinDelayStatus delayStatus;
6255	uint32		old_buf_state;
6256
6257	Assert(!BufferIsLocal(BufferDescriptorGetBuffer(desc)));
6258
6259	init_local_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6260
6261	while (true)
6262	{
6263		/* set BM_LOCKED flag */
6264		old_buf_state = pg_atomic_fetch_or_u32(&desc->state, BM_LOCKED);
6265		/* if it wasn't set before we're OK */
6266		if (!(old_buf_state & BM_LOCKED))
6267			break;
6268		perform_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6269	}
6270	finish_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6271	return old_buf_state | BM_LOCKED;
6272}
6273
6274/*
6275 * Wait until the BM_LOCKED flag isn't set anymore and return the buffer's
6276 * state at that point.
6277 *
6278 * Obviously the buffer could be locked by the time the value is returned, so
6279 * this is primarily useful in CAS style loops.
6280 */
6281static uint32
6282WaitBufHdrUnlocked(BufferDesc *buf)
6283{
6284	SpinDelayStatus delayStatus;
6285	uint32		buf_state;
6286
6287	init_local_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6288
6289	buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&buf->state);
6290
6291	while (buf_state & BM_LOCKED)
6292	{
6293		perform_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6294		buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&buf->state);
6295	}
6296
6297	finish_spin_delay(&delayStatus);
6298
6299	return buf_state;
6300}
6301
6302/*
6303 * BufferTag comparator.
6304 */
6305static inline int
6306buffertag_comparator(const BufferTag *ba, const BufferTag *bb)
6307{
6308	int			ret;
6309	RelFileLocator rlocatora;
6310	RelFileLocator rlocatorb;
6311
6312	rlocatora = BufTagGetRelFileLocator(ba);
6313	rlocatorb = BufTagGetRelFileLocator(bb);
6314
6315	ret = rlocator_comparator(&rlocatora, &rlocatorb);
6316
6317	if (ret != 0)
6318		return ret;
6319
6320	if (BufTagGetForkNum(ba) < BufTagGetForkNum(bb))
6321		return -1;
6322	if (BufTagGetForkNum(ba) > BufTagGetForkNum(bb))
6323		return 1;
6324
6325	if (ba->blockNum < bb->blockNum)
6326		return -1;
6327	if (ba->blockNum > bb->blockNum)
6328		return 1;
6329
6330	return 0;
6331}
6332
6333/*
6334 * Comparator determining the writeout order in a checkpoint.
6335 *
6336 * It is important that tablespaces are compared first, the logic balancing
6337 * writes between tablespaces relies on it.
6338 */
6339static inline int
6340ckpt_buforder_comparator(const CkptSortItem *a, const CkptSortItem *b)
6341{
6342	/* compare tablespace */
6343	if (a->tsId < b->tsId)
6344		return -1;
6345	else if (a->tsId > b->tsId)
6346		return 1;
6347	/* compare relation */
6348	if (a->relNumber < b->relNumber)
6349		return -1;
6350	else if (a->relNumber > b->relNumber)
6351		return 1;
6352	/* compare fork */
6353	else if (a->forkNum < b->forkNum)
6354		return -1;
6355	else if (a->forkNum > b->forkNum)
6356		return 1;
6357	/* compare block number */
6358	else if (a->blockNum < b->blockNum)
6359		return -1;
6360	else if (a->blockNum > b->blockNum)
6361		return 1;
6362	/* equal page IDs are unlikely, but not impossible */
6363	return 0;
6364}
6365
6366/*
6367 * Comparator for a Min-Heap over the per-tablespace checkpoint completion
6368 * progress.
6369 */
6370static int
6371ts_ckpt_progress_comparator(Datum a, Datum b, void *arg)
6372{
6373	CkptTsStatus *sa = (CkptTsStatus *) a;
6374	CkptTsStatus *sb = (CkptTsStatus *) b;
6375
6376	/* we want a min-heap, so return 1 for the a < b */
6377	if (sa->progress < sb->progress)
6378		return 1;
6379	else if (sa->progress == sb->progress)
6380		return 0;
6381	else
6382		return -1;
6383}
6384
6385/*
6386 * Initialize a writeback context, discarding potential previous state.
6387 *
6388 * *max_pending is a pointer instead of an immediate value, so the coalesce
6389 * limits can easily changed by the GUC mechanism, and so calling code does
6390 * not have to check the current configuration. A value of 0 means that no
6391 * writeback control will be performed.
6392 */
6393void
6394WritebackContextInit(WritebackContext *context, int *max_pending)
6395{
6396	Assert(*max_pending <= WRITEBACK_MAX_PENDING_FLUSHES);
6397
6398	context->max_pending = max_pending;
6399	context->nr_pending = 0;
6400}
6401
6402/*
6403 * Add buffer to list of pending writeback requests.
6404 */
6405void
6406ScheduleBufferTagForWriteback(WritebackContext *wb_context, IOContext io_context,
6407							  BufferTag *tag)
6408{
6409	PendingWriteback *pending;
6410
6411	/*
6412	 * As pg_flush_data() doesn't do anything with fsync disabled, there's no
6413	 * point in tracking in that case.
6414	 */
6415	if (io_direct_flags & IO_DIRECT_DATA ||
6416		!enableFsync)
6417		return;
6418
6419	/*
6420	 * Add buffer to the pending writeback array, unless writeback control is
6421	 * disabled.
6422	 */
6423	if (*wb_context->max_pending > 0)
6424	{
6425		Assert(*wb_context->max_pending <= WRITEBACK_MAX_PENDING_FLUSHES);
6426
6427		pending = &wb_context->pending_writebacks[wb_context->nr_pending++];
6428
6429		pending->tag = *tag;
6430	}
6431
6432	/*
6433	 * Perform pending flushes if the writeback limit is exceeded. This
6434	 * includes the case where previously an item has been added, but control
6435	 * is now disabled.
6436	 */
6437	if (wb_context->nr_pending >= *wb_context->max_pending)
6438		IssuePendingWritebacks(wb_context, io_context);
6439}
6440
6441#define ST_SORT sort_pending_writebacks
6442#define ST_ELEMENT_TYPE PendingWriteback
6443#define ST_COMPARE(a, b) buffertag_comparator(&a->tag, &b->tag)
6444#define ST_SCOPE static
6445#define ST_DEFINE
6446#include "lib/sort_template.h"
6447
6448/*
6449 * Issue all pending writeback requests, previously scheduled with
6450 * ScheduleBufferTagForWriteback, to the OS.
6451 *
6452 * Because this is only used to improve the OSs IO scheduling we try to never
6453 * error out - it's just a hint.
6454 */
6455void
6456IssuePendingWritebacks(WritebackContext *wb_context, IOContext io_context)
6457{
6458	instr_time	io_start;
6459	int			i;
6460
6461	if (wb_context->nr_pending == 0)
6462		return;
6463
6464	/*
6465	 * Executing the writes in-order can make them a lot faster, and allows to
6466	 * merge writeback requests to consecutive blocks into larger writebacks.
6467	 */
6468	sort_pending_writebacks(wb_context->pending_writebacks,
6469							wb_context->nr_pending);
6470
6471	io_start = pgstat_prepare_io_time(track_io_timing);
6472
6473	/*
6474	 * Coalesce neighbouring writes, but nothing else. For that we iterate
6475	 * through the, now sorted, array of pending flushes, and look forward to
6476	 * find all neighbouring (or identical) writes.
6477	 */
6478	for (i = 0; i < wb_context->nr_pending; i++)
6479	{
6480		PendingWriteback *cur;
6481		PendingWriteback *next;
6482		SMgrRelation reln;
6483		int			ahead;
6484		BufferTag	tag;
6485		RelFileLocator currlocator;
6486		Size		nblocks = 1;
6487
6488		cur = &wb_context->pending_writebacks[i];
6489		tag = cur->tag;
6490		currlocator = BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&tag);
6491
6492		/*
6493		 * Peek ahead, into following writeback requests, to see if they can
6494		 * be combined with the current one.
6495		 */
6496		for (ahead = 0; i + ahead + 1 < wb_context->nr_pending; ahead++)
6497		{
6498
6499			next = &wb_context->pending_writebacks[i + ahead + 1];
6500
6501			/* different file, stop */
6502			if (!RelFileLocatorEquals(currlocator,
6503									  BufTagGetRelFileLocator(&next->tag)) ||
6504				BufTagGetForkNum(&cur->tag) != BufTagGetForkNum(&next->tag))
6505				break;
6506
6507			/* ok, block queued twice, skip */
6508			if (cur->tag.blockNum == next->tag.blockNum)
6509				continue;
6510
6511			/* only merge consecutive writes */
6512			if (cur->tag.blockNum + 1 != next->tag.blockNum)
6513				break;
6514
6515			nblocks++;
6516			cur = next;
6517		}
6518
6519		i += ahead;
6520
6521		/* and finally tell the kernel to write the data to storage */
6522		reln = smgropen(currlocator, INVALID_PROC_NUMBER);
6523		smgrwriteback(reln, BufTagGetForkNum(&tag), tag.blockNum, nblocks);
6524	}
6525
6526	/*
6527	 * Assume that writeback requests are only issued for buffers containing
6528	 * blocks of permanent relations.
6529	 */
6530	pgstat_count_io_op_time(IOOBJECT_RELATION, io_context,
6531							IOOP_WRITEBACK, io_start, wb_context->nr_pending, 0);
6532
6533	wb_context->nr_pending = 0;
6534}
6535
6536/* ResourceOwner callbacks */
6537
6538static void
6539ResOwnerReleaseBufferIO(Datum res)
6540{
6541	Buffer		buffer = DatumGetInt32(res);
6542
6543	AbortBufferIO(buffer);
6544}
6545
6546static char *
6547ResOwnerPrintBufferIO(Datum res)
6548{
6549	Buffer		buffer = DatumGetInt32(res);
6550
6551	return psprintf("lost track of buffer IO on buffer %d", buffer);
6552}
6553
6554static void
6555ResOwnerReleaseBufferPin(Datum res)
6556{
6557	Buffer		buffer = DatumGetInt32(res);
6558
6559	/* Like ReleaseBuffer, but don't call ResourceOwnerForgetBuffer */
6560	if (!BufferIsValid(buffer))
6561		elog(ERROR, "bad buffer ID: %d", buffer);
6562
6563	if (BufferIsLocal(buffer))
6564		UnpinLocalBufferNoOwner(buffer);
6565	else
6566		UnpinBufferNoOwner(GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1));
6567}
6568
6569static char *
6570ResOwnerPrintBufferPin(Datum res)
6571{
6572	return DebugPrintBufferRefcount(DatumGetInt32(res));
6573}
6574
6575/*
6576 * Helper function to evict unpinned buffer whose buffer header lock is
6577 * already acquired.
6578 */
6579static bool
6580EvictUnpinnedBufferInternal(BufferDesc *desc, bool *buffer_flushed)
6581{
6582	uint32		buf_state;
6583	bool		result;
6584
6585	*buffer_flushed = false;
6586
6587	buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&(desc->state));
6588	Assert(buf_state & BM_LOCKED);
6589
6590	if ((buf_state & BM_VALID) == 0)
6591	{
6592		UnlockBufHdr(desc, buf_state);
6593		return false;
6594	}
6595
6596	/* Check that it's not pinned already. */
6597	if (BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) > 0)
6598	{
6599		UnlockBufHdr(desc, buf_state);
6600		return false;
6601	}
6602
6603	PinBuffer_Locked(desc);		/* releases spinlock */
6604
6605	/* If it was dirty, try to clean it once. */
6606	if (buf_state & BM_DIRTY)
6607	{
6608		LWLockAcquire(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(desc), LW_SHARED);
6609		FlushBuffer(desc, NULL, IOOBJECT_RELATION, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL);
6610		*buffer_flushed = true;
6611		LWLockRelease(BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(desc));
6612	}
6613
6614	/* This will return false if it becomes dirty or someone else pins it. */
6615	result = InvalidateVictimBuffer(desc);
6616
6617	UnpinBuffer(desc);
6618
6619	return result;
6620}
6621
6622/*
6623 * Try to evict the current block in a shared buffer.
6624 *
6625 * This function is intended for testing/development use only!
6626 *
6627 * To succeed, the buffer must not be pinned on entry, so if the caller had a
6628 * particular block in mind, it might already have been replaced by some other
6629 * block by the time this function runs.  It's also unpinned on return, so the
6630 * buffer might be occupied again by the time control is returned, potentially
6631 * even by the same block.  This inherent raciness without other interlocking
6632 * makes the function unsuitable for non-testing usage.
6633 *
6634 * *buffer_flushed is set to true if the buffer was dirty and has been
6635 * flushed, false otherwise.  However, *buffer_flushed=true does not
6636 * necessarily mean that we flushed the buffer, it could have been flushed by
6637 * someone else.
6638 *
6639 * Returns true if the buffer was valid and it has now been made invalid.
6640 * Returns false if it wasn't valid, if it couldn't be evicted due to a pin,
6641 * or if the buffer becomes dirty again while we're trying to write it out.
6642 */
6643bool
6644EvictUnpinnedBuffer(Buffer buf, bool *buffer_flushed)
6645{
6646	BufferDesc *desc;
6647
6648	Assert(BufferIsValid(buf) && !BufferIsLocal(buf));
6649
6650	/* Make sure we can pin the buffer. */
6651	ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
6652	ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
6653
6654	desc = GetBufferDescriptor(buf - 1);
6655	LockBufHdr(desc);
6656
6657	return EvictUnpinnedBufferInternal(desc, buffer_flushed);
6658}
6659
6660/*
6661 * Try to evict all the shared buffers.
6662 *
6663 * This function is intended for testing/development use only! See
6664 * EvictUnpinnedBuffer().
6665 *
6666 * The buffers_* parameters are mandatory and indicate the total count of
6667 * buffers that:
6668 * - buffers_evicted - were evicted
6669 * - buffers_flushed - were flushed
6670 * - buffers_skipped - could not be evicted
6671 */
6672void
6673EvictAllUnpinnedBuffers(int32 *buffers_evicted, int32 *buffers_flushed,
6674						int32 *buffers_skipped)
6675{
6676	*buffers_evicted = 0;
6677	*buffers_skipped = 0;
6678	*buffers_flushed = 0;
6679
6680	for (int buf = 1; buf <= NBuffers; buf++)
6681	{
6682		BufferDesc *desc = GetBufferDescriptor(buf - 1);
6683		uint32		buf_state;
6684		bool		buffer_flushed;
6685
6686		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
6687
6688		buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&desc->state);
6689		if (!(buf_state & BM_VALID))
6690			continue;
6691
6692		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
6693		ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
6694
6695		LockBufHdr(desc);
6696
6697		if (EvictUnpinnedBufferInternal(desc, &buffer_flushed))
6698			(*buffers_evicted)++;
6699		else
6700			(*buffers_skipped)++;
6701
6702		if (buffer_flushed)
6703			(*buffers_flushed)++;
6704	}
6705}
6706
6707/*
6708 * Try to evict all the shared buffers containing provided relation's pages.
6709 *
6710 * This function is intended for testing/development use only! See
6711 * EvictUnpinnedBuffer().
6712 *
6713 * The caller must hold at least AccessShareLock on the relation to prevent
6714 * the relation from being dropped.
6715 *
6716 * The buffers_* parameters are mandatory and indicate the total count of
6717 * buffers that:
6718 * - buffers_evicted - were evicted
6719 * - buffers_flushed - were flushed
6720 * - buffers_skipped - could not be evicted
6721 */
6722void
6723EvictRelUnpinnedBuffers(Relation rel, int32 *buffers_evicted,
6724						int32 *buffers_flushed, int32 *buffers_skipped)
6725{
6726	Assert(!RelationUsesLocalBuffers(rel));
6727
6728	*buffers_skipped = 0;
6729	*buffers_evicted = 0;
6730	*buffers_flushed = 0;
6731
6732	for (int buf = 1; buf <= NBuffers; buf++)
6733	{
6734		BufferDesc *desc = GetBufferDescriptor(buf - 1);
6735		uint32		buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&(desc->state));
6736		bool		buffer_flushed;
6737
6738		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
6739
6740		/* An unlocked precheck should be safe and saves some cycles. */
6741		if ((buf_state & BM_VALID) == 0 ||
6742			!BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&desc->tag, &rel->rd_locator))
6743			continue;
6744
6745		/* Make sure we can pin the buffer. */
6746		ResourceOwnerEnlarge(CurrentResourceOwner);
6747		ReservePrivateRefCountEntry();
6748
6749		buf_state = LockBufHdr(desc);
6750
6751		/* recheck, could have changed without the lock */
6752		if ((buf_state & BM_VALID) == 0 ||
6753			!BufTagMatchesRelFileLocator(&desc->tag, &rel->rd_locator))
6754		{
6755			UnlockBufHdr(desc, buf_state);
6756			continue;
6757		}
6758
6759		if (EvictUnpinnedBufferInternal(desc, &buffer_flushed))
6760			(*buffers_evicted)++;
6761		else
6762			(*buffers_skipped)++;
6763
6764		if (buffer_flushed)
6765			(*buffers_flushed)++;
6766	}
6767}
6768
6769/*
6770 * Generic implementation of the AIO handle staging callback for readv/writev
6771 * on local/shared buffers.
6772 *
6773 * Each readv/writev can target multiple buffers. The buffers have already
6774 * been registered with the IO handle.
6775 *
6776 * To make the IO ready for execution ("staging"), we need to ensure that the
6777 * targeted buffers are in an appropriate state while the IO is ongoing. For
6778 * that the AIO subsystem needs to have its own buffer pin, otherwise an error
6779 * in this backend could lead to this backend's buffer pin being released as
6780 * part of error handling, which in turn could lead to the buffer being
6781 * replaced while IO is ongoing.
6782 */
6783static pg_attribute_always_inline void
6784buffer_stage_common(PgAioHandle *ioh, bool is_write, bool is_temp)
6785{
6786	uint64	   *io_data;
6787	uint8		handle_data_len;
6788	PgAioWaitRef io_ref;
6789	BufferTag	first PG_USED_FOR_ASSERTS_ONLY = {0};
6790
6791	io_data = pgaio_io_get_handle_data(ioh, &handle_data_len);
6792
6793	pgaio_io_get_wref(ioh, &io_ref);
6794
6795	/* iterate over all buffers affected by the vectored readv/writev */
6796	for (int i = 0; i < handle_data_len; i++)
6797	{
6798		Buffer		buffer = (Buffer) io_data[i];
6799		BufferDesc *buf_hdr = is_temp ?
6800			GetLocalBufferDescriptor(-buffer - 1)
6801			: GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
6802		uint32		buf_state;
6803
6804		/*
6805		 * Check that all the buffers are actually ones that could conceivably
6806		 * be done in one IO, i.e. are sequential. This is the last
6807		 * buffer-aware code before IO is actually executed and confusion
6808		 * about which buffers are targeted by IO can be hard to debug, making
6809		 * it worth doing extra-paranoid checks.
6810		 */
6811		if (i == 0)
6812			first = buf_hdr->tag;
6813		else
6814		{
6815			Assert(buf_hdr->tag.relNumber == first.relNumber);
6816			Assert(buf_hdr->tag.blockNum == first.blockNum + i);
6817		}
6818
6819		if (is_temp)
6820			buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&buf_hdr->state);
6821		else
6822			buf_state = LockBufHdr(buf_hdr);
6823
6824		/* verify the buffer is in the expected state */
6825		Assert(buf_state & BM_TAG_VALID);
6826		if (is_write)
6827		{
6828			Assert(buf_state & BM_VALID);
6829			Assert(buf_state & BM_DIRTY);
6830		}
6831		else
6832		{
6833			Assert(!(buf_state & BM_VALID));
6834			Assert(!(buf_state & BM_DIRTY));
6835		}
6836
6837		/* temp buffers don't use BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS */
6838		if (!is_temp)
6839			Assert(buf_state & BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS);
6840
6841		Assert(BUF_STATE_GET_REFCOUNT(buf_state) >= 1);
6842
6843		/*
6844		 * Reflect that the buffer is now owned by the AIO subsystem.
6845		 *
6846		 * For local buffers: This can't be done just via LocalRefCount, as
6847		 * one might initially think, as this backend could error out while
6848		 * AIO is still in progress, releasing all the pins by the backend
6849		 * itself.
6850		 *
6851		 * This pin is released again in TerminateBufferIO().
6852		 */
6853		buf_state += BUF_REFCOUNT_ONE;
6854		buf_hdr->io_wref = io_ref;
6855
6856		if (is_temp)
6857			pg_atomic_unlocked_write_u32(&buf_hdr->state, buf_state);
6858		else
6859			UnlockBufHdr(buf_hdr, buf_state);
6860
6861		/*
6862		 * Ensure the content lock that prevents buffer modifications while
6863		 * the buffer is being written out is not released early due to an
6864		 * error.
6865		 */
6866		if (is_write && !is_temp)
6867		{
6868			LWLock	   *content_lock;
6869
6870			content_lock = BufferDescriptorGetContentLock(buf_hdr);
6871
6872			Assert(LWLockHeldByMe(content_lock));
6873
6874			/*
6875			 * Lock is now owned by AIO subsystem.
6876			 */
6877			LWLockDisown(content_lock);
6878		}
6879
6880		/*
6881		 * Stop tracking this buffer via the resowner - the AIO system now
6882		 * keeps track.
6883		 */
6884		if (!is_temp)
6885			ResourceOwnerForgetBufferIO(CurrentResourceOwner, buffer);
6886	}
6887}
6888
6889/*
6890 * Decode readv errors as encoded by buffer_readv_encode_error().
6891 */
6892static inline void
6893buffer_readv_decode_error(PgAioResult result,
6894						  bool *zeroed_any,
6895						  bool *ignored_any,
6896						  uint8 *zeroed_or_error_count,
6897						  uint8 *checkfail_count,
6898						  uint8 *first_off)
6899{
6900	uint32		rem_error = result.error_data;
6901
6902	/* see static asserts in buffer_readv_encode_error */
6903#define READV_COUNT_BITS	7
6904#define READV_COUNT_MASK	((1 << READV_COUNT_BITS) - 1)
6905
6906	*zeroed_any = rem_error & 1;
6907	rem_error >>= 1;
6908
6909	*ignored_any = rem_error & 1;
6910	rem_error >>= 1;
6911
6912	*zeroed_or_error_count = rem_error & READV_COUNT_MASK;
6913	rem_error >>= READV_COUNT_BITS;
6914
6915	*checkfail_count = rem_error & READV_COUNT_MASK;
6916	rem_error >>= READV_COUNT_BITS;
6917
6918	*first_off = rem_error & READV_COUNT_MASK;
6919	rem_error >>= READV_COUNT_BITS;
6920}
6921
6922/*
6923 * Helper to encode errors for buffer_readv_complete()
6924 *
6925 * Errors are encoded as follows:
6926 * - bit 0 indicates whether any page was zeroed (1) or not (0)
6927 * - bit 1 indicates whether any checksum failure was ignored (1) or not (0)
6928 * - next READV_COUNT_BITS bits indicate the number of errored or zeroed pages
6929 * - next READV_COUNT_BITS bits indicate the number of checksum failures
6930 * - next READV_COUNT_BITS bits indicate the first offset of the first page
6931 *   that was errored or zeroed or, if no errors/zeroes, the first ignored
6932 *   checksum
6933 */
6934static inline void
6935buffer_readv_encode_error(PgAioResult *result,
6936						  bool is_temp,
6937						  bool zeroed_any,
6938						  bool ignored_any,
6939						  uint8 error_count,
6940						  uint8 zeroed_count,
6941						  uint8 checkfail_count,
6942						  uint8 first_error_off,
6943						  uint8 first_zeroed_off,
6944						  uint8 first_ignored_off)
6945{
6946
6947	uint8		shift = 0;
6948	uint8		zeroed_or_error_count =
6949		error_count > 0 ? error_count : zeroed_count;
6950	uint8		first_off;
6951
6952	StaticAssertStmt(PG_IOV_MAX <= 1 << READV_COUNT_BITS,
6953					 "PG_IOV_MAX is bigger than reserved space for error data");
6954	StaticAssertStmt((1 + 1 + 3 * READV_COUNT_BITS) <= PGAIO_RESULT_ERROR_BITS,
6955					 "PGAIO_RESULT_ERROR_BITS is insufficient for buffer_readv");
6956
6957	/*
6958	 * We only have space to encode one offset - but luckily that's good
6959	 * enough. If there is an error, the error is the interesting offset, same
6960	 * with a zeroed buffer vs an ignored buffer.
6961	 */
6962	if (error_count > 0)
6963		first_off = first_error_off;
6964	else if (zeroed_count > 0)
6965		first_off = first_zeroed_off;
6966	else
6967		first_off = first_ignored_off;
6968
6969	Assert(!zeroed_any || error_count == 0);
6970
6971	result->error_data = 0;
6972
6973	result->error_data |= zeroed_any << shift;
6974	shift += 1;
6975
6976	result->error_data |= ignored_any << shift;
6977	shift += 1;
6978
6979	result->error_data |= ((uint32) zeroed_or_error_count) << shift;
6980	shift += READV_COUNT_BITS;
6981
6982	result->error_data |= ((uint32) checkfail_count) << shift;
6983	shift += READV_COUNT_BITS;
6984
6985	result->error_data |= ((uint32) first_off) << shift;
6986	shift += READV_COUNT_BITS;
6987
6988	result->id = is_temp ? PGAIO_HCB_LOCAL_BUFFER_READV :
6989		PGAIO_HCB_SHARED_BUFFER_READV;
6990
6991	if (error_count > 0)
6992		result->status = PGAIO_RS_ERROR;
6993	else
6994		result->status = PGAIO_RS_WARNING;
6995
6996	/*
6997	 * The encoding is complicated enough to warrant cross-checking it against
6998	 * the decode function.
6999	 */
7000#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
7001	{
7002		bool		zeroed_any_2,
7003					ignored_any_2;
7004		uint8		zeroed_or_error_count_2,
7005					checkfail_count_2,
7006					first_off_2;
7007
7008		buffer_readv_decode_error(*result,
7009								  &zeroed_any_2, &ignored_any_2,
7010								  &zeroed_or_error_count_2,
7011								  &checkfail_count_2,
7012								  &first_off_2);
7013		Assert(zeroed_any == zeroed_any_2);
7014		Assert(ignored_any == ignored_any_2);
7015		Assert(zeroed_or_error_count == zeroed_or_error_count_2);
7016		Assert(checkfail_count == checkfail_count_2);
7017		Assert(first_off == first_off_2);
7018	}
7019#endif
7020
7021#undef READV_COUNT_BITS
7022#undef READV_COUNT_MASK
7023}
7024
7025/*
7026 * Helper for AIO readv completion callbacks, supporting both shared and temp
7027 * buffers. Gets called once for each buffer in a multi-page read.
7028 */
7029static pg_attribute_always_inline void
7030buffer_readv_complete_one(PgAioTargetData *td, uint8 buf_off, Buffer buffer,
7031						  uint8 flags, bool failed, bool is_temp,
7032						  bool *buffer_invalid,
7033						  bool *failed_checksum,
7034						  bool *ignored_checksum,
7035						  bool *zeroed_buffer)
7036{
7037	BufferDesc *buf_hdr = is_temp ?
7038		GetLocalBufferDescriptor(-buffer - 1)
7039		: GetBufferDescriptor(buffer - 1);
7040	BufferTag	tag = buf_hdr->tag;
7041	char	   *bufdata = BufferGetBlock(buffer);
7042	uint32		set_flag_bits;
7043	int			piv_flags;
7044
7045	/* check that the buffer is in the expected state for a read */
7046#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
7047	{
7048		uint32		buf_state = pg_atomic_read_u32(&buf_hdr->state);
7049
7050		Assert(buf_state & BM_TAG_VALID);
7051		Assert(!(buf_state & BM_VALID));
7052		/* temp buffers don't use BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS */
7053		if (!is_temp)
7054			Assert(buf_state & BM_IO_IN_PROGRESS);
7055		Assert(!(buf_state & BM_DIRTY));
7056	}
7057#endif
7058
7059	*buffer_invalid = false;
7060	*failed_checksum = false;
7061	*ignored_checksum = false;
7062	*zeroed_buffer = false;
7063
7064	/*
7065	 * We ask PageIsVerified() to only log the message about checksum errors,
7066	 * as the completion might be run in any backend (or IO workers). We will
7067	 * report checksum errors in buffer_readv_report().
7068	 */
7069	piv_flags = PIV_LOG_LOG;
7070
7071	/* the local zero_damaged_pages may differ from the definer's */
7072	if (flags & READ_BUFFERS_IGNORE_CHECKSUM_FAILURES)
7073		piv_flags |= PIV_IGNORE_CHECKSUM_FAILURE;
7074
7075	/* Check for garbage data. */
7076	if (!failed)
7077	{
7078		/*
7079		 * If the buffer is not currently pinned by this backend, e.g. because
7080		 * we're completing this IO after an error, the buffer data will have
7081		 * been marked as inaccessible when the buffer was unpinned. The AIO
7082		 * subsystem holds a pin, but that doesn't prevent the buffer from
7083		 * having been marked as inaccessible. The completion might also be
7084		 * executed in a different process.
7085		 */
7086#ifdef USE_VALGRIND
7087		if (!BufferIsPinned(buffer))
7088			VALGRIND_MAKE_MEM_DEFINED(bufdata, BLCKSZ);
7089#endif
7090
7091		if (!PageIsVerified((Page) bufdata, tag.blockNum, piv_flags,
7092							failed_checksum))
7093		{
7094			if (flags & READ_BUFFERS_ZERO_ON_ERROR)
7095			{
7096				memset(bufdata, 0, BLCKSZ);
7097				*zeroed_buffer = true;
7098			}
7099			else
7100			{
7101				*buffer_invalid = true;
7102				/* mark buffer as having failed */
7103				failed = true;
7104			}
7105		}
7106		else if (*failed_checksum)
7107			*ignored_checksum = true;
7108
7109		/* undo what we did above */
7110#ifdef USE_VALGRIND
7111		if (!BufferIsPinned(buffer))
7112			VALGRIND_MAKE_MEM_NOACCESS(bufdata, BLCKSZ);
7113#endif
7114
7115		/*
7116		 * Immediately log a message about the invalid page, but only to the
7117		 * server log. The reason to do so immediately is that this may be
