7118		 * executed in a different backend than the one that originated the
7119		 * request. The reason to do so immediately is that the originator
7120		 * might not process the query result immediately (because it is busy
7121		 * doing another part of query processing) or at all (e.g. if it was
7122		 * cancelled or errored out due to another IO also failing). The
7123		 * definer of the IO will emit an ERROR or WARNING when processing the
7124		 * IO's results
7125		 *
7126		 * To avoid duplicating the code to emit these log messages, we reuse
7127		 * buffer_readv_report().
7128		 */
7129		if (*buffer_invalid || *failed_checksum || *zeroed_buffer)
7130		{
7131			PgAioResult result_one = {0};
7132
7133			buffer_readv_encode_error(&result_one, is_temp,
7134									  *zeroed_buffer,
7135									  *ignored_checksum,
7136									  *buffer_invalid,
7137									  *zeroed_buffer ? 1 : 0,
7138									  *failed_checksum ? 1 : 0,
7139									  buf_off, buf_off, buf_off);
7140			pgaio_result_report(result_one, td, LOG_SERVER_ONLY);
7141		}
7142	}
7143
7144	/* Terminate I/O and set BM_VALID. */
7145	set_flag_bits = failed ? BM_IO_ERROR : BM_VALID;
7146	if (is_temp)
7147		TerminateLocalBufferIO(buf_hdr, false, set_flag_bits, true);
7148	else
7149		TerminateBufferIO(buf_hdr, false, set_flag_bits, false, true);
7150
7151	/*
7152	 * Call the BUFFER_READ_DONE tracepoint in the callback, even though the
7153	 * callback may not be executed in the same backend that called
7154	 * BUFFER_READ_START. The alternative would be to defer calling the
7155	 * tracepoint to a later point (e.g. the local completion callback for
7156	 * shared buffer reads), which seems even less helpful.
7157	 */
7158	TRACE_POSTGRESQL_BUFFER_READ_DONE(tag.forkNum,
7159									  tag.blockNum,
7160									  tag.spcOid,
7161									  tag.dbOid,
7162									  tag.relNumber,
7163									  is_temp ? MyProcNumber : INVALID_PROC_NUMBER,
7164									  false);
7165}
7166
7167/*
7168 * Perform completion handling of a single AIO read. This read may cover
7169 * multiple blocks / buffers.
7170 *
7171 * Shared between shared and local buffers, to reduce code duplication.
7172 */
7173static pg_attribute_always_inline PgAioResult
7174buffer_readv_complete(PgAioHandle *ioh, PgAioResult prior_result,
7175					  uint8 cb_data, bool is_temp)
7176{
7177	PgAioResult result = prior_result;
7178	PgAioTargetData *td = pgaio_io_get_target_data(ioh);
7179	uint8		first_error_off = 0;
7180	uint8		first_zeroed_off = 0;
7181	uint8		first_ignored_off = 0;
7182	uint8		error_count = 0;
7183	uint8		zeroed_count = 0;
7184	uint8		ignored_count = 0;
7185	uint8		checkfail_count = 0;
7186	uint64	   *io_data;
7187	uint8		handle_data_len;
7188
7189	if (is_temp)
7190	{
7191		Assert(td->smgr.is_temp);
7192		Assert(pgaio_io_get_owner(ioh) == MyProcNumber);
7193	}
7194	else
7195		Assert(!td->smgr.is_temp);
7196
7197	/*
7198	 * Iterate over all the buffers affected by this IO and call the
7199	 * per-buffer completion function for each buffer.
7200	 */
7201	io_data = pgaio_io_get_handle_data(ioh, &handle_data_len);
7202	for (uint8 buf_off = 0; buf_off < handle_data_len; buf_off++)
7203	{
7204		Buffer		buf = io_data[buf_off];
7205		bool		failed;
7206		bool		failed_verification = false;
7207		bool		failed_checksum = false;
7208		bool		zeroed_buffer = false;
7209		bool		ignored_checksum = false;
7210
7211		Assert(BufferIsValid(buf));
7212
7213		/*
7214		 * If the entire I/O failed on a lower-level, each buffer needs to be
7215		 * marked as failed. In case of a partial read, the first few buffers
7216		 * may be ok.
7217		 */
7218		failed =
7219			prior_result.status == PGAIO_RS_ERROR
7220			|| prior_result.result <= buf_off;
7221
7222		buffer_readv_complete_one(td, buf_off, buf, cb_data, failed, is_temp,
7223								  &failed_verification,
7224								  &failed_checksum,
7225								  &ignored_checksum,
7226								  &zeroed_buffer);
7227
7228		/*
7229		 * Track information about the number of different kinds of error
7230		 * conditions across all pages, as there can be multiple pages failing
7231		 * verification as part of one IO.
7232		 */
7233		if (failed_verification && !zeroed_buffer && error_count++ == 0)
7234			first_error_off = buf_off;
7235		if (zeroed_buffer && zeroed_count++ == 0)
7236			first_zeroed_off = buf_off;
7237		if (ignored_checksum && ignored_count++ == 0)
7238			first_ignored_off = buf_off;
7239		if (failed_checksum)
7240			checkfail_count++;
7241	}
7242
7243	/*
7244	 * If the smgr read succeeded [partially] and page verification failed for
7245	 * some of the pages, adjust the IO's result state appropriately.
7246	 */
7247	if (prior_result.status != PGAIO_RS_ERROR &&
7248		(error_count > 0 || ignored_count > 0 || zeroed_count > 0))
7249	{
7250		buffer_readv_encode_error(&result, is_temp,
7251								  zeroed_count > 0, ignored_count > 0,
7252								  error_count, zeroed_count, checkfail_count,
7253								  first_error_off, first_zeroed_off,
7254								  first_ignored_off);
7255		pgaio_result_report(result, td, DEBUG1);
7256	}
7257
7258	/*
7259	 * For shared relations this reporting is done in
7260	 * shared_buffer_readv_complete_local().
7261	 */
7262	if (is_temp && checkfail_count > 0)
7263		pgstat_report_checksum_failures_in_db(td->smgr.rlocator.dbOid,
7264											  checkfail_count);
7265
7266	return result;
7267}
7268
7269/*
7270 * AIO error reporting callback for aio_shared_buffer_readv_cb and
7271 * aio_local_buffer_readv_cb.
7272 *
7273 * The error is encoded / decoded in buffer_readv_encode_error() /
7274 * buffer_readv_decode_error().
7275 */
7276static void
7277buffer_readv_report(PgAioResult result, const PgAioTargetData *td,
7278					int elevel)
7279{
7280	int			nblocks = td->smgr.nblocks;
7281	BlockNumber first = td->smgr.blockNum;
7282	BlockNumber last = first + nblocks - 1;
7283	ProcNumber	errProc =
7284		td->smgr.is_temp ? MyProcNumber : INVALID_PROC_NUMBER;
7285	RelPathStr	rpath =
7286		relpathbackend(td->smgr.rlocator, errProc, td->smgr.forkNum);
7287	bool		zeroed_any,
7288				ignored_any;
7289	uint8		zeroed_or_error_count,
7290				checkfail_count,
7291				first_off;
7292	uint8		affected_count;
7293	const char *msg_one,
7294			   *msg_mult,
7295			   *det_mult,
7296			   *hint_mult;
7297
7298	buffer_readv_decode_error(result, &zeroed_any, &ignored_any,
7299							  &zeroed_or_error_count,
7300							  &checkfail_count,
7301							  &first_off);
7302
7303	/*
7304	 * Treat a read that had both zeroed buffers *and* ignored checksums as a
7305	 * special case, it's too irregular to be emitted the same way as the
7306	 * other cases.
7307	 */
7308	if (zeroed_any && ignored_any)
7309	{
7310		Assert(zeroed_any && ignored_any);
7311		Assert(nblocks > 1);	/* same block can't be both zeroed and ignored */
7312		Assert(result.status != PGAIO_RS_ERROR);
7313		affected_count = zeroed_or_error_count;
7314
7315		ereport(elevel,
7316				errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
7317				errmsg("zeroing %u page(s) and ignoring %u checksum failure(s) among blocks %u..%u of relation \"%s\"",
7318					   affected_count, checkfail_count, first, last, rpath.str),
7319				affected_count > 1 ?
7320				errdetail("Block %u held the first zeroed page.",
7321						  first + first_off) : 0,
7322				errhint_plural("See server log for details about the other %d invalid block.",
7323							   "See server log for details about the other %d invalid blocks.",
7324							   affected_count + checkfail_count - 1,
7325							   affected_count + checkfail_count - 1));
7326		return;
7327	}
7328
7329	/*
7330	 * The other messages are highly repetitive. To avoid duplicating a long
7331	 * and complicated ereport(), gather the translated format strings
7332	 * separately and then do one common ereport.
7333	 */
7334	if (result.status == PGAIO_RS_ERROR)
7335	{
7336		Assert(!zeroed_any);	/* can't have invalid pages when zeroing them */
7337		affected_count = zeroed_or_error_count;
7338		msg_one = _("invalid page in block %u of relation \"%s\"");
7339		msg_mult = _("%u invalid pages among blocks %u..%u of relation \"%s\"");
7340		det_mult = _("Block %u held the first invalid page.");
7341		hint_mult = _("See server log for the other %u invalid block(s).");
7342	}
7343	else if (zeroed_any && !ignored_any)
7344	{
7345		affected_count = zeroed_or_error_count;
7346		msg_one = _("invalid page in block %u of relation \"%s\"; zeroing out page");
7347		msg_mult = _("zeroing out %u invalid pages among blocks %u..%u of relation \"%s\"");
7348		det_mult = _("Block %u held the first zeroed page.");
7349		hint_mult = _("See server log for the other %u zeroed block(s).");
7350	}
7351	else if (!zeroed_any && ignored_any)
7352	{
7353		affected_count = checkfail_count;
7354		msg_one = _("ignoring checksum failure in block %u of relation \"%s\"");
7355		msg_mult = _("ignoring %u checksum failures among blocks %u..%u of relation \"%s\"");
7356		det_mult = _("Block %u held the first ignored page.");
7357		hint_mult = _("See server log for the other %u ignored block(s).");
7358	}
7359	else
7360		pg_unreachable();
7361
7362	ereport(elevel,
7363			errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
7364			affected_count == 1 ?
7365			errmsg_internal(msg_one, first + first_off, rpath.str) :
7366			errmsg_internal(msg_mult, affected_count, first, last, rpath.str),
7367			affected_count > 1 ? errdetail_internal(det_mult, first + first_off) : 0,
7368			affected_count > 1 ? errhint_internal(hint_mult, affected_count - 1) : 0);
7369}
7370
7371static void
7372shared_buffer_readv_stage(PgAioHandle *ioh, uint8 cb_data)
7373{
7374	buffer_stage_common(ioh, false, false);
7375}
7376
7377static PgAioResult
7378shared_buffer_readv_complete(PgAioHandle *ioh, PgAioResult prior_result,
7379							 uint8 cb_data)
7380{
7381	return buffer_readv_complete(ioh, prior_result, cb_data, false);
7382}
7383
7384/*
7385 * We need a backend-local completion callback for shared buffers, to be able
7386 * to report checksum errors correctly. Unfortunately that can only safely
7387 * happen if the reporting backend has previously called
7388 * pgstat_prepare_report_checksum_failure(), which we can only guarantee in
7389 * the backend that started the IO. Hence this callback.
7390 */
7391static PgAioResult
7392shared_buffer_readv_complete_local(PgAioHandle *ioh, PgAioResult prior_result,
7393								   uint8 cb_data)
7394{
7395	bool		zeroed_any,
7396				ignored_any;
7397	uint8		zeroed_or_error_count,
7398				checkfail_count,
7399				first_off;
7400
7401	if (prior_result.status == PGAIO_RS_OK)
7402		return prior_result;
7403
7404	buffer_readv_decode_error(prior_result,
7405							  &zeroed_any,
7406							  &ignored_any,
7407							  &zeroed_or_error_count,
7408							  &checkfail_count,
7409							  &first_off);
7410
7411	if (checkfail_count)
7412	{
7413		PgAioTargetData *td = pgaio_io_get_target_data(ioh);
7414
7415		pgstat_report_checksum_failures_in_db(td->smgr.rlocator.dbOid,
7416											  checkfail_count);
7417	}
7418
7419	return prior_result;
7420}
7421
7422static void
7423local_buffer_readv_stage(PgAioHandle *ioh, uint8 cb_data)
7424{
7425	buffer_stage_common(ioh, false, true);
7426}
7427
7428static PgAioResult
7429local_buffer_readv_complete(PgAioHandle *ioh, PgAioResult prior_result,
7430							uint8 cb_data)
7431{
7432	return buffer_readv_complete(ioh, prior_result, cb_data, true);
7433}
7434
7435/* readv callback is passed READ_BUFFERS_* flags as callback data */
7436const PgAioHandleCallbacks aio_shared_buffer_readv_cb = {
7437	.stage = shared_buffer_readv_stage,
7438	.complete_shared = shared_buffer_readv_complete,
7439	/* need a local callback to report checksum failures */
7440	.complete_local = shared_buffer_readv_complete_local,
7441	.report = buffer_readv_report,
7442};
7443
7444/* readv callback is passed READ_BUFFERS_* flags as callback data */
7445const PgAioHandleCallbacks aio_local_buffer_readv_cb = {
7446	.stage = local_buffer_readv_stage,
7447
7448	/*
7449	 * Note that this, in contrast to the shared_buffers case, uses
7450	 * complete_local, as only the issuing backend has access to the required
7451	 * datastructures. This is important in case the IO completion may be
7452	 * consumed incidentally by another backend.
7453	 */
7454	.complete_local = local_buffer_readv_complete,
7455	.report = buffer_readv_report,
7456};
